fact: December 5 is exactly 360 days after January 10 - that's a perfect circle in degrees!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when many squirrels start getting super chunky because they're growing their thickest winter fur coats.
The sun sets before 5 PM in most of Canada and northern US states around this date, making it feel like bedtime when you're still doing homework!
December 5 falls smack in the middle of Sagittarius season - the archer constellation that looks like a teapot in the sky.
In Antarctica, penguin chicks are hatching right now because it's early summer down there!
This date is exactly 16 days before winter solstice in the north, which means daylight is shrinking by about 2 minutes every single day.
Many evergreen trees are pumping extra sugar into their needles around this time to keep from freezing solid like popsicles.
The moon looks extra bright and crisp on cold December nights because there's less water vapor in the winter air to blur it.
In northern places, the sun rises so far south that it can shine through windows that stay dark all summer long.
December 5 is when many bears in cold regions are entering their deepest sleep phase and their heart rate drops to just 8 beats per minute!
In Australia and South America, December 5 is when many flowers are blooming their brightest because summer is just getting started!
